target.name: Vertical Semiconductor
target.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vertical Semiconductor is a Seed-stage company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector that has raised $11M to date. The sector universe includes 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with notable private-stage companies including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using the $11M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $28M. The Peer-Median method cannot be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within the Chips & Semiconductors sector—while 14 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 meet both stage and valuation-tier filters simultaneously. Public-Comp Anchoring is not applicable because the target's revenue is not available; the public semiconductor universe trades at a median P/S of 20.3x, but this multiple cannot be deployed without revenue data.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $23M–$32M, with a median of $28M. The primary uncertainty is the reliance on a single quantitative method. Given Vertical Semiconductor's Seed stage and the absence of revenue disclosure, corroboration through additional peer data or financial metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
